explanations.8: The optional chaining operator (?.) safely checks if siteConfig exists before accessing customFields, returning undefined if it doesn't rather than throwing an error.
explanations.18: This condition checks if either piece of required data is missing; if so, it stops the fetch and marks loading as false to prevent trying to call the API.
explanations.25: The `at://` protocol is Bluesky's internal URI format for identifying posts; it's being constructed from the account handle and post key to create a unique post identifier.
explanations.26: encodeURIComponent() escapes special characters in the URI so it's safe to pass as a URL parameter without breaking the query string.
explanations.34: After the API returns the full post thread data, this line extracts just the like and repost counts from the nested post object for easier access.
explanations.48: These dependencies tell React to re-run the effect whenever either the record key or handle changes, ensuring the stats stay up-to-date if the post ID changes.
